---
title: "Expectation-Maximization (EM) Algorithm for Gaussian Mixture Models"
author: "PhD Applicant"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

## Overview

This document implements the Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm to estimate the parameters (means, variances, and mixing probabilities) of a 1D Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM). This demonstrates a foundational Frequentist approach to finite mixture models with latent variables.

## Algorithm Implementation

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
set.seed(42)
```

```{r em-algorithm}
fit_gmm_em <- function(X, K, max_iter = 100, tol = 1e-6) {
  n <- length(X)
  
  # 1. Initialization
  pi_k <- rep(1/K, K)
  mu <- sample(X, K, replace = FALSE)
  sigma2 <- rep(var(X), K)
  
  log_likelihoods <- numeric()
  
  for (it in 1:max_iter) {
    # -------------------------
    # E-step: Calculate Responsibilities (gamma)
    # -------------------------
    gamma <- matrix(0, nrow = n, ncol = K)
    for (k in 1:K) {
      gamma[, k] <- pi_k[k] * dnorm(X, mean = mu[k], sd = sqrt(sigma2[k]))
    }
    
    # Compute log-likelihood
    log_lik <- sum(log(rowSums(gamma)))
    log_likelihoods <- c(log_likelihoods, log_lik)
    
    # Normalize responsibilities
    gamma <- gamma / rowSums(gamma)
    
    # -------------------------
    # M-step: Update Parameters
    # -------------------------
    N_k <- colSums(gamma) # Effective number of points
    
    pi_k <- N_k / n
    mu <- colSums(gamma * X) / N_k
    
    for (k in 1:K) {
      sigma2[k] <- sum(gamma[, k] * (X - mu[k])^2) / N_k[k]
    }
    
    # -------------------------
    # Check Convergence
    # -------------------------
    if (it > 1 && abs(log_likelihoods[it] - log_likelihoods[it-1]) < tol) {
      cat(sprintf("Converged at iteration %d\n", it))
      break
    }
  }
  
  return(list(pi = pi_k, mu = mu, sigma2 = sigma2, log_liks = log_likelihoods))
}
```

## Example Usage

```{r run-em}
# Generate synthetic bimodal data
data <- c(rnorm(150, mean = 0, sd = 1), rnorm(150, mean = 5, sd = 1.5))

# Fit the model
results <- fit_gmm_em(data, K = 2)

cat("Estimated Means:", results$mu, "\n")
cat("Estimated Variances:", results$sigma2, "\n")
cat("Estimated Mixing Probs:", results$pi, "\n")
```
